---
title: "Placeholder 01"
---

This is a placeholder for part of the website

## Setup
```{r}
#| label: setup

library(fredr)
library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)

fredr_set_key(Sys.getenv("FRED_API_KEY"))
```

## Unemployment Rate
```{r}
#| label: unemployment

us_u <- fredr("UNRATE")                %>% mutate(geo = "United States")
ca_u <- fredr("CAUR")                  %>% mutate(geo = "California")
vc_u <- fredr("LAUCN061110000000003A") %>% mutate(geo = "Ventura County")

unemp <- bind_rows(us_u, ca_u, vc_u) %>%
  select(date, value, geo) %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2000-01-01"))

p_unemp <- ggplot(unemp, aes(x = date, y = value, color = geo)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "United States"  = "#1f77b4",
    "California"     = "#ff7f0e",
    "Ventura County" = "#2ca02c"
  )) +
  labs(
    title    = "Unemployment Rate (%)",
    subtitle = "Click legend to isolate a region | Hover for values",
    x = NULL, y = "Unemployment Rate (%)", color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

ggplotly(p_unemp, tooltip = c("x", "y", "color")) %>%
  layout(hovermode = "x unified")
```

## JOLTS — Job Openings, Hires, Quits & Layoffs
```{r}
#| label: jolts

jolts_series <- c(
  "JTSJOL" = "Job Openings",
  "JTSHIL" = "Hires",
  "JTSQUL" = "Quits",
  "JTSLDL" = "Layoffs & Discharges"
)

jolts_data <- map_dfr(names(jolts_series), function(id) {
  fredr(id) %>%
    mutate(
      series = jolts_series[[id]],
      value  = value / 1000
    )
}) %>%
  select(date, value, series) %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2005-01-01"))

p_jolts <- ggplot(jolts_data, aes(x = date, y = value, color = series)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title    = "JOLTS: Job Openings, Hires, Quits & Layoffs (millions)",
    subtitle = "United States only — state & county level data not available for JOLTS",
    x = NULL, y = "Count (millions)", color = NULL,
    caption  = "Source: BLS Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey via FRED"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

ggplotly(p_jolts, tooltip = c("x", "y", "color")) %>%
  layout(hovermode = "x unified")
```

## Unemployment Rate: US vs CA vs Ventura County
```{r}
#| label: vc-chart

recent_unemp <- unemp %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2010-01-01"))

p_recent <- ggplot(recent_unemp, aes(x = date, y = value, color = geo)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "United States"  = "#1f77b4",
    "California"     = "#ff7f0e",
    "Ventura County" = "#2ca02c"
  )) +
  labs(
    title    = "Unemployment Rate: US vs CA vs Ventura County",
    subtitle = "2010–present | Click legend to isolate",
    x = NULL, y = "Unemployment Rate (%)", color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

ggplotly(p_recent, tooltip = c("x", "y", "color")) %>%
  layout(hovermode = "x unified")
```

## Latest Snapshot
```{r}
#| label: latest-table

latest <- recent_unemp %>%
  group_by(geo) %>%
  slice_max(date, n = 1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(value) %>%
  rename(
    Geography        = geo,
    `Latest Date`    = date,
    `Unemployment %` = value
  )

knitr::kable(latest, digits = 1)
```

## GDP Growth (Indexed Comparison)
```{r}
#| label: gdp-comparison

vc_gdp <- fredr("GDPALL06111") %>% mutate(geo = "Ventura County")
ca_gdp <- fredr("CANGSP")      %>% mutate(geo = "California")
us_gdp <- fredr("GDPA")        %>% mutate(geo = "United States", value = value * 1000)

gdp_all <- bind_rows(vc_gdp, ca_gdp, us_gdp) %>%
  select(date, value, geo) %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2001-01-01")) %>%
  group_by(geo) %>%
  mutate(indexed = value / first(value) * 100) %>%
  ungroup()

p_gdp <- ggplot(gdp_all, aes(x = date, y = indexed, color = geo)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "United States"  = "#1f77b4",
    "California"     = "#ff7f0e",
    "Ventura County" = "#2ca02c"
  )) +
  labs(
    title    = "GDP Growth — Indexed to 2001 (100 = Starting Point)",
    subtitle = "Allows fair comparison across different-sized economies | Annual",
    x = NULL, y = "Index (2001 = 100)", color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

ggplotly(p_gdp, tooltip = c("x", "y", "color")) %>%
  layout(hovermode = "x unified")
```

## Per Capita Personal Income
```{r}
#| label: income-comparison

vc_inc <- fredr("PCPI06111")       %>% mutate(geo = "Ventura County")
ca_inc <- fredr("CAPCPI")          %>% mutate(geo = "California")
us_inc <- fredr("A792RC0A052NBEA") %>% mutate(geo = "United States")

income_all <- bind_rows(vc_inc, ca_inc, us_inc) %>%
  select(date, value, geo) %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2000-01-01"))

p_income <- ggplot(income_all, aes(x = date, y = value, color = geo)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "United States"  = "#1f77b4",
    "California"     = "#ff7f0e",
    "Ventura County" = "#2ca02c"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::dollar) +
  labs(
    title    = "Per Capita Personal Income",
    subtitle = "US vs California vs Ventura County | Annual",
    x = NULL, y = "Dollars per person", color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

ggplotly(p_income, tooltip = c("x", "y", "color")) %>%
  layout(hovermode = "x unified")
```

## Population Growth (Indexed Comparison)
```{r}
#| label: population-comparison

vc_pop <- fredr("CAVENT2POP") %>% mutate(geo = "Ventura County", value = value * 1000)
ca_pop <- fredr("CAPOP")      %>% mutate(geo = "California",     value = value * 1000)
us_pop <- fredr("POPTHM")     %>% mutate(geo = "United States",  value = value * 1000)

pop_all <- bind_rows(vc_pop, ca_pop, us_pop) %>%
  select(date, value, geo) %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2000-01-01")) %>%
  group_by(geo) %>%
  mutate(indexed = value / first(value) * 100) %>%
  ungroup()

p_pop <- ggplot(pop_all, aes(x = date, y = indexed, color = geo)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "United States"  = "#1f77b4",
    "California"     = "#ff7f0e",
    "Ventura County" = "#2ca02c"
  )) +
  labs(
    title    = "Population Growth — Indexed to 2000 (100 = Starting Point)",
    subtitle = "Is Ventura County growing faster or slower than the state and nation?",
    x = NULL, y = "Index (2000 = 100)", color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

ggplotly(p_pop, tooltip = c("x", "y", "color")) %>%
  layout(hovermode = "x unified")
```

## Nonfarm Payroll Employment
```{r}
#| label: payroll-all

us_pay <- fredr("PAYEMS")    %>% mutate(geo = "United States")
ca_pay <- fredr("CANA")      %>% mutate(geo = "California")
vc_pay <- fredr("OXNA106NA") %>% mutate(geo = "Ventura County (MSA)")

payroll_all <- bind_rows(us_pay, ca_pay, vc_pay) %>%
  select(date, value, geo) %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2000-01-01")) %>%
  group_by(geo) %>%
  mutate(indexed = value / first(value) * 100) %>%
  ungroup()

p_payroll <- ggplot(payroll_all, aes(x = date, y = indexed, color = geo)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "United States"        = "#1f77b4",
    "California"           = "#ff7f0e",
    "Ventura County (MSA)" = "#2ca02c"
  )) +
  labs(
    title    = "Nonfarm Payroll Employment — Indexed to 2000 (100 = Starting Point)",
    subtitle = "Ventura County MSA (Oxnard-Thousand Oaks-Ventura) vs CA vs US | Monthly",
    x = NULL, y = "Index (2000 = 100)", color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

ggplotly(p_payroll, tooltip = c("x", "y", "color")) %>%
  layout(hovermode = "x unified")
```
